interpretation: Annotator agreement on the four-class taxonomy. Below 0.70 means the four classes are not reliably distinguishable.
Overall Cohen's kappa between two independent annotators. Below 0.45 means annotators agree mostly by chance.
Krippendorff's alpha as a redundancy check; reported but not a primary pass/fail criterion.
Per-boundary kappa for the mild_vs_strong boundary. Below 0.40 triggers taxonomy revision (see downgrade criteria 1).
Per-boundary kappa for strong_vs_contradiction (detail-swap detection).
Per-boundary kappa for supported_vs_mild; this is the weakest silver-stage boundary and is reported as informational.
Rate of unsure labels. High unsure rate indicates the decision tree is not operational enough.
Overall agreement between silver labels and adjudicated gold labels.
Fraction of silver strong_action labels that remain strong_action under gold. Below 0.50 triggers downgrade criterion 2.
Number of silver mild cases that gold re-labels as strong. Indicates silver under-escalation.
Number of silver strong cases that gold re-labels as mild. Indicates silver over-escalation.
Fraction of silver contradiction_candidate labels that remain contradiction under gold.
Macro-F1 of the flat four-class baseline on the gold subset.
Strong-action precision of the flat baseline.
Strong-action recall of the flat baseline.
Strong-action F1 of the flat baseline.
Contradiction F1 of the flat baseline.
False positives per true positive for the flat baseline.
